summary: Large tydisplay/ToyDsp decomp PR that replaced many placeholder stubs in src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c with typed C, recovered display/archive/JObj helper structs, and tightened related headers/callsites. decomp.dev reported +5476 matched bytes (+0.14% total matched code), with 8 new full matches and 15 additional tydisplay improvements. The PR body only said it was a tracking PR, so intent beyond matching progress is inferred from the diff and bot report.
what_changed: Implemented or substantially advanced many tydisplay functions, including un_8031830C, un_80318714, un_80318B1C, un_80318CB4, un_80319540, un_80319994, un_80319EF0, fn_8031A4EC, fn_8031A94C, un_8031B1FC, un_8031B328, un_8031B460_OnEnter, un_8031B850, un_8031BA78, un_8031BC54, un_8031BF34, un_8031C1D0, un_8031C454, un_8031C5E4, and un_8031C8B8.
decomp.dev reported 8 new full matches in main/melee/ty/tydisplay: fn_8031A94C, fn_8031A4EC, un_8031C5E4, un_8031C454, un_8031B850, un_8031C1D0, un_8031C8B8, and un_8031BA78.

Added recovered display data types and archive/name-table helpers in tydisplay.c, including TyDspBaseData, TyDspArchiveHolder, TyDspBgData, TyDspArchNames, TyDspNameTables, TyDspSceneGfx, TyDspGrid, TyDspConfig, and TyDspPos.
Added TySortElem to tydisplay.h and replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes using s32, f32, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, and TyDspEntry.
Added TyDspEntry to ty/types.h with a 0x10-byte STATIC_ASSERT, and changed tylist.h globals un_804D6EAC and un_804D6EB0 from void* to TyDspEntry*.
Changed un_8031B9DC to return TyDspEntry* and rewrote table walking from raw 0x10-byte pointer arithmetic to typed TyDspEntry iteration.
Changed un_8031C354 to take an s32 buffer pointer instead of s32 (*)[], then updated it_8027CC88 to use s32 sp1C[10] and natural &sp1C[temp_r3] calls.
Changed un_8031C454 to take the selected display id and return s32; Ground_801C5878 now calls un_8031C454(temp_r30) before saving stage_info.x6E4[0].
Corrected toy.h prototype for un_80305D00 from unknown params/return to float un_80305D00(void), matching its use as controller/stick input in fn_8031A4EC and fn_8031A94C.
Final diff incorporated review cleanup around asserts: raw __assert condition "0" cases became HSD_ASSERT line-number calls, and an OSPanic site used __FILE__.
smart_moves: Recovering TyDspEntry early made multiple functions cleaner at once: un_8031B9DC can return typed entries, un_8031C354 compares entry->x04, tylist.h exposes typed table globals, and item code no longer needs array-pointer casts.
The PR kept uncertain display-state structs local to tydisplay.c while still adding enough field layout to match archive loading, camera setup, JObj transforms, and cleanup code.

Using TyDspArchNames/TyDspNameTables allowed 43-entry archive, JObj, and matanim name tables to be copied as structured locals, matching codegen patterns visible in un_8031BC54, un_8031C454, and un_8031C5E4.
A shared inline quicksort helper let the two sort wrapper symbols un_8031830C and un_80318714 share C logic while preserving the required public symbols.
The PR fixed dependent prototypes and callsites in ground.c, it_2725.c, toy.h, tydisplay.h, tylist.h, and types.h in the same slice, avoiding a header-only mismatch.
Reviewer comments were applied in the final diff: HSD_ASSERT replaced hand-written __assert("tydisplay.c", line, "0") style code where appropriate.
decomp_lessons: When a recovered function signature changes, update all affected callsites immediately; un_8031C454 needed both the typed prototype and Ground_801C5878 passing temp_r30.
For stack buffers passed to recovered APIs, prefer the true element type and pointer form; s32 sp1C[10] plus &sp1C[temp_r3] was cleaner than casting to s32 (*)[].
Typed data-table recovery can unlock both matching and readability: TyDspEntry converted magic offsets x00/x04/x08/x0C into consistent field accesses while keeping unknown semantics honest.
Use HSD_ASSERT(line, condition) for HSD assert sites whose string is just condition "0"; this matched reviewer guidance and avoids unnecessary file/string literals.
For OSPanic sites, reviewer guidance was to use __FILE__ and declare/use the real string because it helps matching and later linking.
Distinguish full matches from partial improvements in records: this PR added 8 full matches but also left several implemented functions as high-percent unmatched improvements.
naming_conventions: Display-specific recovered structs use the TyDsp prefix: TyDspEntry, TyDspGrid, TyDspConfig, TyDspBgData, TyDspArchNames, TyDspNameTables, TyDspSceneGfx, and TyDspPos.
When semantics are not yet known, fields stay offset-based, such as x00, x04, x05, x08, x0C, pad_06, x04_min_x, x08_min_z, x0C_max_x, and x10_max_z.
Small helper sort records can use semantic names when clear: TySortElem has key and val.

Headers should replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with precise prototypes once callsites establish types, using forward declarations for HSD_GObj and HSD_JObj rather than pulling full headers into public headers.
Existing lifecycle suffix conventions were preserved for entrypoints such as un_803182D4_OnFrame, un_8031B9A4_OnFrame, and un_8031B460_OnEnter.
Address-based names like un_804D6F10, un_804D6F14, un_804D6F18, un_803B8988, and un_803B8A34 were retained where stronger semantic names were not proven.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in several routines, including quicksort/layout/camera code, to shape stack frames for matching.
Preserved odd-looking sort behavior by casting TyDspPos storage to TySortElem* where the original code sorts 8-byte records through the same key/value layout.
Preserved bit-level comparisons where needed, such as comparing *(s32*)&sort[n].val in one sort path rather than forcing a high-level float comparison.
Used explicit goto labels and small scoped blocks in layout and setup routines to control register lifetimes and branch structure.
Kept HSD archive/JObj loading calls close to original string-table access patterns, including HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, lbArchive_LoadSymbols, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, and un_80306A48.
Introduced a small inline helper un_8031C5E4_inline for archive public-address lookup, likely to match call/inlining behavior in un_8031C5E4.
review_feedback: PsiLupan requested replacing raw __assert sites with HSD_ASSERT, noting the assert string appeared to be just condition 0.
A second assert site received the same HSD_ASSERT guidance.
For an OSPanic site, PsiLupan asked to replace the filename with __FILE__ and declare/use the actual string because it makes matching and linking the file later easier.
PsiLupan also noted that a static string did not need to be declared above and should be used directly in the actual function call.
follow_up_queries: Later PRs that rename or document TyDspEntry fields x00 x04 x05 x08 x0C
Remaining tydisplay partial matches after PR 2297: un_80318CB4 un_80319540 un_80319994 un_80319EF0 un_8031B460_OnEnter
HSD_ASSERT versus __assert review guidance in melee decomp PRs
un_8031C354 s32 buffer callsites and item it_8027CC88 cleanup
ToyDsp archive name tables un_803B8988 un_803B8A34 and HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress matching
